finding: Visible quantity cues on a stimulus bias perception and behavior in the direction of the cue; effect persists despite corrective verbal information and varies with cognitive load on attention to the cue.

Intensive multi-component instruction combining phonics, text reading, and spelling improves reading outcomes in children at risk; adaptive software provides no advantage over fixed-difficulty software.

Pairing of a neutral stimulus with a personally-significant unconditioned stimulus elicits larger event-related positivity and changes in oscillatory brain activity; effect emerges without conscious recognition of the unconditioned stimulus.

Perceived government impartiality toward ethnic groups is associated with higher likelihood of sustained economic growth; effect is robust to controls for democracy, fractionalization, and inequality.
Manufacturing flexibility is jointly determined by process simplicity and procedural discipline; effect is significant and replicable across countries and industries.

Repeated exposure to or checking of a stimulus diminishes memory confidence, vividness, and detail; effect magnitude is equal for exposure without checking as for checking with exposure, and confidence remains reduced after a delay.
